title: Dynamic programming and graph thinking
summary: Recognize overlapping subproblems, memoize repeated work, and model relationships as nodes plus edges.

solution.summary: It avoids recomputing the same subproblem by storing previously computed answers and reusing them later.

hints: One part represents entities, the other relationships.
items.prompt: What are the two basic ingredients of a graph model?

solution.summary: Graphs represent entities as nodes (or vertices) and relationships between them as edges.

items.prompt: Implement `fib(n, memo=None)` so it computes Fibonacci numbers using memoization.

solution.summary: Use the reference solution to compare structure, not just syntax.

items.prompt: You are choosing features for a small app under a time budget. How would a knapsack-style framing help you make that decision?

solution.summary: Knapsack thinking turns an ambiguous prioritization problem into an optimization problem with explicit tradeoffs.
checklist: List candidate items with value and cost.
Explain why brute force becomes expensive as choices grow.
